categories.name: Unauthorised Restrictive Practice
categories.legislative_source: NDIS (Restrictive Practices and Behaviour Support) Rules 2018; NDIS (Incident Management and Reportable Incidents) Rules 2018

mandatory_steps.action: Ensure immediate safety of the participant and others
Cease the unauthorised restrictive practice as soon as it is safe to do so
Notify supervisor or manager immediately
Document the incident in the internal Incident Register
Notify the NDIS Commission via the My Reportable Incidents portal within 5 business days (or 24 hours if serious harm occurred)
Consult with a specialist behaviour support practitioner to review or develop a Behaviour Support Plan

categories.reporting_timeframe: 5 business days (24 hours if serious harm)
required_documentation: Notice of a Reportable Incident Form

additional_steps.action: Notify the Victorian Senior Practitioner via the Restrictive Intervention Data System (RIDS) within 1 business day for emergency use
additional_steps.detail: Emergency use of a restrictive practice must be reported to the Senior Practitioner via RIDS within 1 business day.

additional_steps.action: Authorised Program Officer (APO) must review the use of the restrictive practice
additional_steps.detail: The APO must review the incident to ensure it was the least restrictive option and refer for reassessment.

categories.name: Medication Error / Missed Medication
categories.legislative_source: NDIS Practice Standards - Core Module 4 (Provision of Supports Environment); NDIS (Incident Management and Reportable Incidents) Rules 2018

mandatory_steps.action: Acknowledge the error and stay calm
Check the participant's vital signs and monitor for adverse reactions
Contact a medical professional (doctor, pharmacist, or 000 if emergency) for advice
Follow the medical advice provided
Notify supervisor or manager immediately
Document the error on the Medication Administration Record (MAR) chart and complete an internal incident report
If the error caused serious harm, notify the NDIS Commission within 24 hours

categories.reporting_timeframe: Internal reporting immediately; NDIS Commission within 24 hours if serious harm

categories.name: Injury / Fall / Medical Emergency
categories.legislative_source: NDIS (Incident Management and Reportable Incidents) Rules 2018; Work Health and Safety Act 2011

mandatory_steps.action: Provide immediate first aid or call 000 for emergency medical assistance
Ensure the environment is safe to prevent further injury
Notify supervisor or manager immediately
Notify family, guardian, or emergency contact
Document the incident in the internal Incident Register
If it is a serious injury, notify the NDIS Commission within 24 hours
If it meets the definition of a notifiable incident under WHS Act, notify the state WHS regulator immediately and preserve the site

categories.reporting_timeframe: 24 hours for NDIS Commission (if serious); Immediately for WHS regulator (if notifiable)
required_documentation: Notice of a Reportable Incident Form

additional_steps.action: Notify WorkSafe Victoria immediately if it is a notifiable incident
additional_steps.detail: Under the OHS Act 2004 (Vic), WorkSafe must be notified immediately of incidents resulting in death or serious injury.

categories.name: Abuse / Neglect / Exploitation Indicators
categories.legislative_source: NDIS (Incident Management and Reportable Incidents) Rules 2018; NDIS Code of Conduct

mandatory_steps.action: Ensure the immediate safety of the participant and separate them from the alleged perpetrator
Provide immediate medical or psychological support if required
Contact Police (000) if a crime is suspected or there is immediate danger
Notify supervisor or manager immediately
Preserve any evidence related to the alleged abuse or neglect
Notify the NDIS Commission via the My Reportable Incidents portal within 24 hours
Provide the participant with information about access to independent advocates

required_documentation: Notice of a Reportable Incident Form

categories.name: Missing Consent / Capacity Concerns
categories.legislative_source: NDIS Practice Standards - Core Module 1 (Rights and Responsibilities); NDIS Code of Conduct

mandatory_steps.action: Pause the support or intervention if consent is unclear or withdrawn
Explain the support/intervention using the participant's preferred language and mode of communication
Allow the participant sufficient time to consider their options and seek advice
If capacity is still a concern, involve the participant's nominated decision-maker, guardian, or advocate
Document the consent process, the participant's response, and any decisions made in the progress notes
Notify supervisor if the lack of consent prevents essential care or poses a risk

categories.reporting_timeframe: Internal reporting via progress notes; escalate immediately if risk of harm

additional_steps.action: Consult the Office of the Public Advocate (OPA) if a guardian needs to be appointed
additional_steps.detail: If the participant lacks capacity for a significant decision and has no guardian, an application to VCAT may be required.

categories.name: Property Damage / Financial Irregularity
categories.legislative_source: NDIS Practice Standards - Core Module 4 (Participant Money and Property); Australian Consumer Law

mandatory_steps.action: Ensure the immediate safety of the participant and others if property damage involves a hazard
Document the damage or financial discrepancy immediately with clear details (and photos if applicable)
Notify supervisor or manager immediately
Complete an internal incident report detailing the property damage or financial irregularity
If theft or fraud is suspected, contact the Police
If the incident involves financial abuse or exploitation by a worker, notify the NDIS Commission within 24 hours

categories.reporting_timeframe: Internal reporting immediately; 24 hours for NDIS Commission if financial abuse

categories.name: Behavioural Incident Requiring Reporting
categories.legislative_source: NDIS (Restrictive Practices and Behaviour Support) Rules 2018; NDIS Practice Standards

mandatory_steps.action: Implement proactive and reactive strategies as outlined in the participant's Behaviour Support Plan (BSP)
Ensure the safety of the participant, staff, and others in the environment
If the behaviour results in serious injury or involves an unauthorised restrictive practice, notify supervisor immediately
Document the behaviour, triggers, and strategies used in the progress notes and/or behaviour tracking sheet
Complete an internal incident report if the behaviour caused harm, property damage, or required emergency intervention
Notify the NDIS Commission if the incident meets the criteria for a reportable incident (e.g., serious injury, unauthorised RP)

categories.reporting_timeframe: Internal reporting by end of shift; NDIS Commission within 24h/5 days if reportable criteria met

additional_steps.action: Report to Victorian Senior Practitioner via RIDS if a regulated restrictive practice was used
additional_steps.detail: Must be reported monthly, or within 1 business day if it was an emergency use.

categories.name: Worker Safety / WHS Concerns
categories.legislative_source: Work Health and Safety Act 2011; NDIS Practice Standards

mandatory_steps.action: Remove yourself and others from immediate danger
Seek immediate first aid or medical attention if injured
Notify supervisor or manager immediately
Complete an internal incident/hazard report detailing the safety concern or injury
If it is a 'notifiable incident' (death, serious injury/illness, dangerous incident), the PCBU must notify the state WHS regulator immediately
Preserve the incident site until an inspector arrives or directs otherwise (for notifiable incidents)

categories.reporting_timeframe: Internal reporting immediately; WHS regulator immediately for notifiable incidents

additional_steps.action: Notify WorkSafe Victoria immediately for notifiable incidents
additional_steps.detail: Under the OHS Act 2004 (Vic), employers must notify WorkSafe immediately after becoming aware of a notifiable incident.

categories.name: Environmental / WHS Hazards
categories.legislative_source: Work Health and Safety Act 2011; NDIS Practice Standards - Core Module 4 (Safe Environment)

mandatory_steps.action: Identify and assess the environmental hazard
Take immediate action to eliminate or minimise the risk (e.g., clean up spill, block access)
If the hazard cannot be immediately resolved, isolate the area and warn others
Notify supervisor or manager about the hazard
Complete an internal hazard report form
Follow up to ensure the hazard has been permanently resolved

categories.reporting_timeframe: Internal reporting by end of shift or immediately if high risk

categories.name: Participant Rights Violations
categories.legislative_source: NDIS Practice Standards - Core Module 1 (Rights and Responsibilities); NDIS Code of Conduct

mandatory_steps.action: Intervene immediately if you witness a violation of a participant's rights (e.g., loss of dignity, lack of privacy, denied choice)
Support the participant and acknowledge their feelings and rights
Notify supervisor or manager immediately
Document the incident objectively in an internal incident report
Provide the participant with information on how to make a complaint and access an independent advocate
If the violation constitutes abuse, neglect, or exploitation, notify the NDIS Commission within 24 hours

categories.reporting_timeframe: Internal reporting immediately; 24 hours for NDIS Commission if it constitutes abuse/neglect

categories.name: Service Delivery Failures
categories.legislative_source: NDIS Practice Standards - Core Module 3 (Provision of Supports); NDIS Code of Conduct

mandatory_steps.action: Identify the failure (e.g., missed shift, late arrival, incorrect support provided)
Take immediate action to ensure the participant's essential needs (e.g., medication, meals, personal care) are met
Communicate with the participant (and family/guardian) about the failure and the steps being taken to resolve it
Notify supervisor or rostering team immediately to arrange alternative support
Document the service failure and the actions taken in the progress notes and internal incident system
If the failure resulted in harm or neglect, notify the NDIS Commission within 24 hours

categories.reporting_timeframe: Internal reporting immediately; 24 hours for NDIS Commission if it constitutes neglect

categories.name: Cultural Safety Concerns
categories.legislative_source: NDIS Practice Standards - Core Module 1 (Individual Values and Beliefs); NDIS Code of Conduct

mandatory_steps.action: Acknowledge the concern and apologise if a culturally unsafe practice occurred
Adjust the support delivery immediately to respect the participant's culture, values, and beliefs
Discuss the participant's cultural needs and preferences with them to ensure future supports are culturally safe
Notify supervisor or manager to ensure the participant's support plan is updated with cultural requirements
Document the cultural safety concern and the participant's preferences in the progress notes
Provide information on how to make a complaint if the participant wishes to do so

categories.reporting_timeframe: Internal reporting by end of shift
external_notifications: None (unless escalated to a formal complaint)

categories.name: Pricing Fraud / Billing Irregularity
categories.legislative_source: NDIS Code of Conduct (Element 8); Australian Consumer Law; NDIS Pricing Arrangements and Price Limits

mandatory_steps.action: Identify the billing irregularity (e.g., overcharging, charging for unprovided services, wrong price limit)
Do not process or approve the incorrect charge; hold the invoice for review
Notify supervisor, manager, or the finance/billing department immediately
Document the discrepancy clearly, keeping copies of relevant timesheets, invoices, and service agreements
Communicate transparently with the participant or plan manager about the error and the steps to rectify it
If intentional fraud is suspected, management must report it to the NDIA Fraud Reporting and Investigations team

categories.reporting_timeframe: Internal reporting immediately; NDIA reporting as soon as fraud is suspected

categories.name: Unresolved Complaint / Grievance
categories.legislative_source: NDIS Practice Standards - Core Module 2B (Complaints Management); NDIS Code of Conduct

mandatory_steps.action: Listen to the participant's complaint actively and without defensiveness
Attempt to resolve the issue immediately at the point of service if possible and appropriate
If unresolved, provide the participant with the organisation's formal complaints procedure and offer assistance to complete it
Inform the participant of their right to access an independent advocate and their right to complain directly to the NDIS Commission
Notify supervisor or the designated Complaints Officer about the grievance
Document the complaint, the participant's desired outcome, and actions taken in the complaints register

categories.reporting_timeframe: Acknowledge complaint promptly (usually within 24-48 hours internally)
